Medicare Glossary

100+ Medicare terms explained in plain English.

These plain-English definitions are educational and simplified — exact rules and amounts can vary by plan and year. For specifics, see Medicare.gov or ask a licensed agent.

Why the words matter

Medicare terminology directly shapes your coverage choices. The difference between a copay and coinsurance, knowing what counts as creditable coverage, or understanding a plan's maximum out-of-pocket can mean hundreds of dollars a year and which plan actually fits you. Use this glossary as you compare plans — and reach out anytime a term is still fuzzy. There are no silly questions in Medicare.

Questions about Medicare?

If a term still doesn't make sense, Bret will explain it in plain English — free, no pressure.

Call or Text Bret Schedule a Free Medicare Review

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Medicare Part B?
Medical insurance that covers doctor visits, outpatient care, preventive services, and durable medical equipment, for a standard monthly premium.
What is a deductible?
The amount you pay out of pocket for covered services before your plan begins to pay.
What is IRMAA?
An income-related surcharge added to your Part B and Part D premiums if your income is above a set threshold.
What is a formulary?
The list of prescription drugs a plan covers, organized into pricing tiers.
What is a Special Enrollment Period?
A window outside the usual enrollment periods when you can change coverage after a qualifying event, such as losing employer coverage.
